title: Pilosocereus aureispinus
taxonomy: Pilosocereus aureispinus is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae. The genus Pilosocereus is part of the tribe Cacteae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other cacti species. Its classification helps in understanding the evolutionary history and diversity of the cactus family.

intro: Pilosocereus aureispinus, also known as the golden spine cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of Pilosocereus aureispinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosocereus aureispinus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
